prevalence.trend: increasing with diabetes burden and delayed diagnosis

incidence.value: 2-5% per year among people with diabetes

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited foot care access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical neuropathy assessment with monofilament/vibration testing; evaluate reversible contributors (B12 deficiency, thyroid disease, alcohol use)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; foot screening often incomplete outside structured diabetes clinics
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; high ulcer/amputation risk when foot care is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Screening concentrated in urban diabetes clinics; peripheral centers rely on clinical exam
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Glycemic control, foot care education, neuropathic pain meds (pregabalin, duloxetine, amitriptyline), prompt ulcer management
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited podiatry and diabetic foot services, high out-of-pocket costs, and delayed ulcer care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large diabetes programs and availability of affordable generics for neuropathic pain
